promptEn: A floating log in warm water may not be wood at all. Eyes and nose can stay above the surface while most of the body hides. Patience and sudden jaws make the trap work.
hintJp: 水面に目と鼻だけを出して、丸太のようにじっと待つ爬虫類です。

promptEn: I am not a bird, although I can fly. In the dark, sound can help me find small moving meals. My wings are made from skin stretched over long fingers.
hintJp: 鳥ではありませんが飛べて、暗い場所で音を使って虫などを探す動物です。

promptEn: Calling me only a meat eater would miss part of the clue. I may eat berries, honey, or fish. Some kinds rest for a long time when the cold season arrives.
hintJp: 魚や木の実なども食べ、寒い季節に長く休む種類もいる大きな動物です。

promptEn: Feathers are the strongest clue, even when some kinds cannot fly well. Many of us build nests and lay eggs. A beak helps us eat without teeth.
hintJp: 羽、くちばし、巣、卵が手がかりになる動物の仲間です。

promptEn: I can step quietly before a small animal hears me. My whiskers help me judge narrow spaces. Even when I live with people, my hunting habits still show.
hintJp: ひげでせまい場所を感じ取り、静かに動くのが得意な動物です。

promptEn: My nose can follow a story written in smells. I may guard a home, guide a person, or run beside a human friend. A moving tail often tells how I feel.
hintJp: においをたどるのが得意で、人間の友だちになることが多い動物です。

promptEn: I live in the sea, but I am not a breathing-with-gills animal. I come to the surface for air and may move with others in a group. Clicks and whistles can help me understand the watery world.
hintJp: 海にすみますが、えらではなく空気を吸い、音を使って仲間と動く動物です。

promptEn: I can reach leaves without climbing a tree. My nose can drink, smell, lift, and spray water. Smaller animals may move away when my heavy feet shake the ground.
hintJp: 長い鼻を手のように使い、とても大きな体を持つ動物です。

promptEn: Legs are not needed in my usual home. I move by bending my body and using fins. Instead of breathing air the way people do, I take what I need from water.
hintJp: 水の中でひれを使って泳ぎ、えらで呼吸する動物です。

promptEn: My life may begin in water with a tail. Later, I can leap on land and catch insects with a sticky tongue. After rain, my voice may join a noisy pond chorus.
hintJp: 子どもの時は水中で育ち、成長するとジャンプして虫を捕る動物です。

promptEn: I can eat leaves that many grassland animals cannot reach. My height comes from more than one famous body part. Brown patches help break up my shape among trees and sunlight.
hintJp: 長い首と高い足で、他の動物が届かない木の葉を食べられる動物です。

promptEn: Walking slowly is not my best way to travel. Strong back legs help me cover ground with long jumps. A baby can ride in a pouch while it grows.
hintJp: 強い後ろ足でジャンプして進み、赤ちゃんをお腹のふくろで育てる動物です。

promptEn: My voice can travel far across open grassland. I often live with a group, and teamwork can help during a hunt. A grown male may carry a thick collar of hair.
hintJp: 草原で大きな声を響かせ、オスにはたてがみがある肉食動物です。

promptEn: Hands help me hold fruit, branches, and sometimes tools. I often learn by watching others in my group. A tail may help with balance, but my cleverness is also important.
hintJp: 手で枝や果物をつかみ、群れで学びながら生活することが多い動物です。

promptEn: Darkness is not a big problem for me. My large eyes and quiet wings help me hunt when many other animals rest. A mouse may be in danger before it hears me coming.
hintJp: 夜に活動し、大きな目と静かな翼で小さな獲物をねらう鳥です。

promptEn: Feathers do not always mean skill in the sky. My wings work better like paddles in cold water. I may look slow on ice, but I become a fast hunter below the surface.
hintJp: 羽はありますが空を飛ばず、冷たい水の中で速く泳ぐ鳥です。

promptEn: I do not win by fighting large hunters. My long ears notice danger early, and my quick feet help me escape. A hidden hole can be safer than open grass.
hintJp: 長い耳で危険に気づき、すばやく走って穴に逃げる小さな動物です。

promptEn: I cross grass and branches without feet. My tongue gathers clues from the air. Some kinds squeeze tightly, while others use venom to stop prey.
hintJp: 足がなく、舌で周りを感じ取り、毒やしめつけで獲物を捕る種類もいます。

promptEn: I carry my protection instead of wearing a borrowed house. Some of my relatives swim well, while others move slowly on land. When danger comes, hiding can be smarter than running.
hintJp: かたい甲羅を持ち、危険な時に体を守ることができる動物です。

promptEn: I am huge and live in the ocean, but I am not a giant gill-breather. I must rise to the surface for air. Some kinds sing across long distances under the waves.
hintJp: 海にすむとても大きな動物で、えらではなく空気を吸い、歌う種類もいます。
